review.reference: Review passed: 4 descriptive signals (resting_hr_status [status], resting_hr_trend/steps_trend/weight_trend [trend]) implemented and LIVE via engine.compute. Independently confirmed: _ensure_builtin_signals_loaded() registers all four (no KeyError through compute), and import stays lazy (REGISTRY empty, module not imported on import). Wiring is the sanctioned one-line addition of premura.engine.descriptive_signals to WP01-owned _BUILTIN_SIGNAL_MODULES. Shared _query.py helpers (load_metric_policy/latest_usable_value/ordered_window) reused by all signals, no duplicated warehouse logic, no dead helpers. Freshness honesty verified: stale values flagged STALE never relabeled current. steps (missing_data_policy:none) yields ZERO imputed points -> gaps stay gaps; carry-forward gated centrally on policy.carries_forward so steps cannot inherit LOCF. weight LOCF carry-forward gated on validity_window so 20-day-old reading is STALE/gaps not current. Tests cover status current/stale/no-value, trend clear/sparse/insufficient, steps gaps, weight carried-forward+stale; driven through engine.compute on temp DuckDB. Tests: descriptive_signals 11 pass, engine 8 pass, -k engine 35 pass, full suite 106 pass. No out-of-scope changes.

Ready for review: two comparative signals (own-baseline + before/after) with sufficiency honesty and bounded caveats; wired into builtin loader
Started review via action command

review.reference: Review passed: Two comparative signals implemented and registered (sleep_deep_pct_baseline=baseline family, hrv_change_around_date=change family); both LIVE through engine.compute (no KeyError) and lazy-loaded (REGISTRY empty + module unimported on plain engine import, verified). Own-baseline logic is strictly user-relative (mean of user's own prior obs in last 30d, excludes latest; UNKNOWN+caveat when <3 prior values; no population/clinical/profile refs). LANGUAGE-BOUNDARY AUDIT: every clinical/statistical term in the module is either a docstring/comment or an explicit NEGATIVE disclaimer; baseline caveat explicitly says 'device/vendor estimate ... not a clinical or medical threshold'; change disclaimer explicitly says 'does not test statistical significance and does not imply that anything on that date caused a change'. NO affirmative p-values/CIs/significance/causal language anywhere. before/after primitives gate sufficiency (>=3 each side) and ChangeAroundDateResult.validate() enforces delta/means=None when insufficient, so no fabricated delta. ANCHOR-DESIGN ASSESSMENT: midpoint-default anchor (for compute resolution) is acceptable - it runs through the same sufficiency gate + standing disclaimer, never presents a confident/causal change; the explicit-anchor public fn hrv_change_around_date(conn, anchor_date) is clean and directly tested (sufficient/insufficient/no-data), ready for WP04's MCP tool. Tests: 10 WP03 + 8 engine + 45 engine-keyed all pass. Diff scoped to exactly the 3 sanctioned files (comparative_signals.py, test file, one-line __init__ wiring).

Ready for review: six signal-backed MCP tools routing through engine, three raw tools preserved, structured unavailable-state serialization, nine tools published
Started review via action command

review.reference: Review passed: BOUNDARY ENFORCEMENT verified — independent grep of signal-backed section (server.py line 180+) found ZERO raw SQL (no SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/FROM hp./fact_measurement/fact_interval/conn.execute/dim_metric); all six wrappers route through engine.compute() and comparative_signals.hrv_change_around_date(). Raw SQL confined to pre-existing list_metrics/metric_summary. _open_warehouse extraction is behavior-preserving (same read_only=True path + try/finally close) for query_warehouse; raw-tool contract unchanged. FOUR-STATE serialization structurally distinct via status field: available / missing_input (family==missing_input OR freshness==unavailable) / stale_input (freshness==stale) / insufficient_data (sufficient_data False, trend_direction==unknown, or comparison_state==unknown) — not collapsed into generic error. NINE tools confirmed published through FastMCP entrypoint (3 raw + 6 new). hrv_change_around_date passes user-supplied parsed anchor to explicit-anchor engine path (not midpoint default), verified by test asserting anchor_date passthrough. Tests cover registration=9, one success per family, missing/stale, insufficient-data x2, preserved raw tools, public entrypoint reachability. test_mcp_server.py out-of-scope edit is STRICTLY membership-list expansion 3->9 (new _EXPECTED_TOOLS constant); NO raw-tool behavior assertion weakened/removed — forced-by-design and acceptable. Validation: test_mcp_signal_tools+test_mcp_server 26 passed; full suite 131 passed.
